response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack web developer, but misses nearly every hard requirement for this role: no Japanese language proficiency (native-level required), no embedded networking or LAN-switch experience, no Linux kernel investigation/modification work, and no security-technology background (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption). C experience is limited to a brief 7-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago; her career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. The profile does not fit an embedded security firmware position.
strengths: Long, consistent software engineering career (15+ years) with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and light embedded/hardware work (robotic arms, audio systems)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
No experience with embedded networking, L2/L3 switching, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel modification
C for UNIX is not a core skill; recent work is entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js)

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering experience and Linux/systems exposure
